section_title: DETAILS OF THE UNDERLYING SCENARIOS USED TO CALCULATE THE IMPACTS

scenarios.title: A1 - raw material supply
scenarios.description: This module takes into account the extraction and processing of all raw materials (natural gypsum, recycled gypsum, additives, paper,…), energy and packaging which occur upstream to the manufacturing site.

scenarios.title: A2 – transport to the manufacturer
scenarios.description: This module includes the transportation of raw materials and packaging to the manufacturing site. The modelling includes road, boat and/or train transportations.

scenarios.title: A3 - manufacturing
scenarios.description: This module includes the manufacture of products and the manufacture of packaging. The production of packaging material is considered at this stage. The processing of any waste arising from this stage is also included.

scenarios.title: A4 – transport to the building site
scenarios.description:
Plasterboards are categorized as 'loose products' in table 6 of the B-PCR. The following transport steps apply:
– 40% directly to the construction site over 100 km with a 16-32 ton lorry
– 60% to a supplier over 100 km with a 16 -32 ton lorry
– 85% of these 60% is transported over 35 km from supplier to construction site with a 16-32 ton lorry
– 15% of these 60% is transported over 35 km from supplier to construction site with a 7.5-16 ton lorry

Fuel type and consumption of vehicle or vehicle type used for transport:
Truck: >32 Ton - Diesel: 0.16l/km (default data ecoinvent)
Truck: 16-32 Ton - Diesel: 0.03 l/km (default data ecoinvent)
Truck: 7.5-16 Ton - Diesel: 0.4 l/km (default data ecoinvent)

Distance (km): 60, 69.75, 5.25
Capacity utilisation (including empty returns): Default data from ecoinvent
Bulk density of transported products: 824 kg/m3
Volume capacity utilization factor: 1

scenarios.title: A5 – installation in the building
scenarios.description:
At the construction site, packaging materials are released. Also 5% material losses have been taken into account

Processes necessary for the installation of the product:
– Cutting the plasterboard to size (by hand),
– Fixing the plasterboard on the framing with screws..
– Filling the joint between plasterboards with jointing compound reinforced by jointing tape.

Fixation materials: 8 pcs Screws
Jointing materials: 0.33 kg Jointing compound, 1.23 m Jointing tape
Treatments: -
Material losses: 5% Plasterboard
Packaging: 0.22 kg Wooden pallet
Ancillary materials for installation (specified by material): Insert information
Water use: 0.105 litres
Other resource use: none
Quantitative description of energy type (regional mix) and consumption during the installation process: none
Waste materials on the building site before waste processing, generated by the product's installation (specified by type): 0.515 (kg) scrap plasterboard
Output materials (specified by type) as result of waste processing at the building site e.g. of collection for recycling, for energy recovery, disposal (specified by route):
– 5% of plasterboard : 20% recycling / 80% landfil
– Wooden pallet: re-used 7 times
Direct emissions to ambient air, soil and water: None
Distance: 50 km to landfill, 30 km to recycling

scenarios.title: B – use stage (excluding potential savings)
scenarios.description:
Description of the stage:
the use stage is divided into the following modules:
• B1: Use
• B2: Maintenance
• B3: Repair
• B4: Replacement
• B5: Refurbishment
• B6: Operational energy use
• B7: Operational water use

Description of the scenarios and additional technical information:
The product has a reference service life of 60 years. This assumes that the product will last in situ with no requirements for maintenance, repair, replacement, or refurbishment throughout this period. Therefore, it has no impact at this stage.

scenarios.title: C: End of life
scenarios.description:
Description of the stage:
this stage includes the next modules:
• C1: Deconstruction, demolition: The de-construction and/or dismantling of the product take part of the demolition of the entire building. In our case, a small amount of energy is considered 0.05 MJ/m².
• C2: Transport to waste processing
we used standard eco invent data according B-EPD pcr
• C3: Waste processing for reuse, recovery and/or recycling
• C4: disposal, including provision and all transport, provision of all materials, products and related energy and water use

Module C2 – Transport to waste processing
Type of vehicle: Truck 16-32 ton
Fuel consumption: Diesel 0.03 l/km (default data from ecoinvent)
Distance: 50km (landfill), 30km (recycling), 100km (incineration)
Capacity utilisation: Default data from ecoinvent
Density of products: 824 kg/m3
Assumptions: Same truck for all the scenarios of end-of-life

End-of-life modules – C3 and C4
Wastes collected separately: 2.06 kg (20 % of plasterboard)
Wastes collected as mixed construction waste: 0
Waste for re-use: none
Waste for recycling: 1.99 kg (20% of gypsum)
Waste for energy recovery: 0.07 kg (20% of paperliner)
Waste for final disposal: 8.58 kg (80% of plasterboard and 100% of auxiliaries (eg: jointing compounds, tape))

scenarios.title: D – Benefits and loads beyond the system boundaries
scenarios.description:
In the module D is declared the environmental benefits from reusable products, recyclable materials, or energy recovery. Module D considers product raw materials, and relevant packaging valorisation in recycling and/or in incineration with energy recovery.

Quantitative description of the loads beyond the system boundaries:
The processing of recycled materials, such as gypsum and paper liner

Quantitative description of the benefits beyond the system boundaries:
The recycled material from the gypsum and the energy recovery from incineration of paper
